headline: WHO declares international public health emergency over Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
summary: The World Health Organization has declared an international public health emergency due to an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has likely killed over 130 people and sickened more than 600. The outbreak involves a rare strain of the virus.

headline: China blocks Pentagon official's Beijing visit over $14 billion US arms package for Taiwan
summary: Beijing is holding up a proposed visit by the Pentagon's top policy official as China pressures the Trump administration over a $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan.

headline: Air France and Airbus found guilty of manslaughter over 2009 plane crash
summary: A French court found Air France and Airbus guilty of manslaughter over the 2009 air disaster which killed 228 people. The crash occurred during a flight from Rio de Janeiro to Paris.

headline: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo has cancelled its World Cup training camp due to the ongoing Ebola outbreak. The outbreak, caused by a rare Ebola species, is thought to have caused 139 deaths so far.

headline: Iran displays weapons at mass wedding ceremony amid war tensions
summary: In Tehran, weapons demonstrations including a 'Khaybar-buster' missile display at a mass wedding ceremony send signals both at home and abroad as the threat of war with Israel and the US remains.

headline: Gaza flotilla activists face deportation from Israel amid international backlash
summary: Activists have begun leaving Israel after international outrage over their treatment in detention. Netanyahu scolded the Israeli security minister for releasing videos taunting detained activists.

headline: Gonorrhoea and syphilis hit record levels in Europe
summary: Sexually transmitted infections have surged to record levels in Europe, driven by record cases and gaps in testing and prevention, according to a European health agency report.

headline: Mali jihadists enforce fuel blockade, dozens of vehicles burnt
summary: Jihadist groups in Mali have enforced a fuel blockade, burning dozens of vehicles. Mali is a landlocked country and depends heavily on fuel transported from neighbouring countries.

headline: Trump says he will speak to Taiwan's president
summary: President Trump announced he will speak with Taiwan's president, a move likely to increase tensions with China amid the ongoing dispute over US arms sales to the island.

headline: Nvidia's record results fail to impress investors amid competition concerns
summary: The chip giant reported stellar quarterly results but its shares fell after-hours as investors question whether it can maintain its growth pace amid increasing competition in the AI chip market.
